rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOLITA G. RIOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling: Grant. The will contest by Contestant/Plaintiff De Guzman is based on 3 grounds. This tentative ruling identifies the 3 grounds and specifies the reasons for the tentative ruling and identifies the evidence on which the tentative ruling relies as follows: 1. Lack of testamentary capacity. Moving party's separate statement Fact #4 is supported by the declaration of John Lank. Opposing party's reference to the Lank declaration as self serving does not create a triable issue. Opposing party submits no competent evidence that testator lacked testamentary capacity. 2. Lack of execution. Moving party's separate statement Fact #2 is undisputed and is supported by Exhibit A. In addition, evidence in support of moving party's Fact #4 is the declaration of John Lank. Opposing party's reference to the Lank declaration as self serving does not create a triable issue. 3. Undue influence. Moving party's separate statement Facts #5 & #6 are supported by the declaration of Nora Gavino at paragraphs 4 and 5. Opposing party's disputed evidence to Fact #5 does not create a triable issue of material fact based on the legal requirements for undue influence. Opposing party's dispute to Fact #6 does not include opposition evidence and does not create a triable issue.
